patient.diagnosis: Post-cardiac stent placement, Hypertension, Atrial Fibrillation

medications.name: Aspirin
medications.purpose: prevents blood clots from forming in your heart stent
medications.special_instructions: do not stop taking this without talking to Dr. Patel — stopping suddenly can cause a heart attack

medications.name: Clopidogrel
medications.purpose: works with aspirin to prevent the stent from becoming blocked
medications.special_instructions: take at the same time each day; do not skip doses

medications.name: Metoprolol
medications.purpose: controls your heart rate and reduces strain on your heart
medications.special_instructions: do not stop suddenly — taper off only under doctor supervision

medications.name: Atorvastatin
medications.purpose: lowers your cholesterol to protect the stent and prevent future blockages
medications.special_instructions: avoid eating grapefruit or drinking grapefruit juice while on this medication

activity_restrictions: no strenuous activity or heavy lifting for 2 weeks
short gentle walks are encouraged — start with 10 minutes and gradually increase
no driving for 48 hours after the procedure
avoid vigorous exercise until cleared by Dr. Patel
stop any activity immediately if you feel chest pain, pressure, or shortness of breath
wound_care: keep the catheter insertion site (wrist or groin) clean and dry for 48 hours
a small bruise or firmness at the insertion site is normal
do not rub or scratch the site — let it heal on its own
if bleeding or significant swelling occurs at the site, apply firm pressure and call 911

follow_ups.purpose: post-procedure cardiac check and medication review
ECG and lipid panel to assess heart function and cholesterol

warning_signs: chest pain, pressure, tightness, or squeezing — call 911 immediately
shortness of breath or difficulty breathing
swelling in your legs or ankles
bleeding or large bruising at the catheter insertion site that does not stop
